tool_args.label: New York City Transit Key Performance Metrics - March 2026

payload.title: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.description: Performance metrics document prepared for the New York City Transit & Bus Committee meeting on March 23rd, 2026

evidence_refs.source_quote: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
March 2026
This performance metrics document was prepared for the New York City Transit & Bus Committee meeting.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Operates all subways and buses in New York City

evidence_refs.source_quote: This performance metrics document was prepared for the New York City Transit & Bus Committee meeting.
New York City Transit and MTA Bus operate all subways and buses in New York City.

payload.description: North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000-square-mile travel area

evidence_refs.source_quote: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000-square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

payload.title: President of New York City Transit

evidence_refs.source_quote: Demetrius Crichlow President New York City Transit

evidence_refs.source_quote: The MTA works closely with the New York City Department of Transportation (DOT), the New York City Department of Finance (DOF), and trusted industry partners
We continue to work closely with the New York City Department of Transportation to expand dedicated bus lanes and busways

payload.description: Primary policing agency in the NYCT subway system

evidence_refs.source_quote: As the primary policing agency in the NYCT subway system, the NYPD continues to enforce laws, rules, and regulations within the transit system.
New York City Transit and MTA Bus operate all subways and buses in New York City.

evidence_refs.source_quote: The MTA works closely with the New York City Department of Transportation (DOT), the New York City Department of Finance (DOF), and trusted industry partners
NYC Department of Finance receives and processes the payment of the fine or adjudicates any dispute made by the violating vehicle's owner.
EVP of Buses, Frank Farrell

payload.description: Accessibility upgrades at Sheepshead Bay B Q Station including two new elevators, new entrance with three turnstiles and two exit gates, two new staircases, ADA-compliant raised boarding areas, yellow tactile edge strips, flood-resistant construction with improved drainage

evidence_refs.source_quote: 119 Year-Old Sheepshead Bay Made Fully Accessible
The MTA is one step closer to delivering on its goal of an accessible system for all New Yorkers with the completion of station and accessibility upgrades at Sheepshead Bay B Q Station in Brooklyn.

payload.description: Started as Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) in 2019, evolved into ACE. Uses cameras on buses to enforce bus lanes, bus stops, and double-parking rules. Active on 58 routes, covering about 560 miles of streets across all five boroughs.

evidence_refs.source_quote: To tackle this problem, the MTA established the Automated Bus Lane Enforcement (ABLE) program in 2019.
What began as a focus on bus lane enforcement has evolved into a broader initiative known as Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE).
Today, automated camera enforcement is active on 58 routes, covering about 560 miles of streets and across all five boroughs.

payload.description: Expansion of Convo Access ASL interpreter pilot from 13 to 68 locations across the MTA system, beginning April 2026. Includes all 30 Customer Service Centers in the subway and 38 commuter railroad stations.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Earlier this month, the MTA announced the expansion of a successful pilot, Convo Access.
This expansion, which will begin in April, will more than quintuple the locations of Convo Access from 13 customers service locations to 68 across the MTA system.
Service Delivered was 88.8%, down 4.8 percentage points from February 2025 and 6.3 percentage points from January 2026.
Bus speeds decreased to 8.0 mph, down 3.6 percent from last year and 4.8 percent from the prior month.
average weekday subway ridership showed a year-over-year decline for the first time since the pandemic, albeit a nominal decline of 0.4%, with 3.89 million riders compared to 3.91 million in 2025

tool_args.label: Bus February 2026 Weekday Paid Ridership Change

evidence_refs.source_quote: Average weekday paid bus ridership saw a 17% decline from 2025.
total ridership increased by 2%, when compared to February 2025

ACE when combined with dedicated bus lanes and street upgrades has helped improve bus speeds across the full length of ACE-enabled routes, with some segments on ACE routes achieving gains of nearly 30 percent.

tool_args.label: ACE Active on Q60 and B12 Routes

payload.claim_text: ACE recently activated on Q60 and B12 routes, more than one million customers across 58 routes now benefit from this technology.
payload.subject: ACE program

evidence_refs.source_quote: With the recent activation of ACE on the Q60 and B12, more than one million customers across 58 routes now benefit from this technology.

warnings: claim.routes is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Route arrays should link to route records when supported. Suggested relation: route has_claim claim when the route endpoint resolves exactly.

payload.claim_text: Future ACE program expansion includes enforcement of bicycle lanes, expected to begin in 2026.
payload.subject: ACE program

evidence_refs.source_quote: The future of the program includes adding additional use cases, including the enforcement of bicycle lanes, expecting to begin in 2026.

tool_args.label: MetroCard Retirement and Coin Payment End

payload.claim_text: In 2026, the MTA is anticipating the full retirement of MetroCard, the end of Coin Payment on buses, and the introduction of a new proof of payment system.
payload.subject: MTA fare system changes

evidence_refs.source_quote: This year we're anticipating the full retirement of MetroCard, the end of Coin Payment on buses, and the introduction of our new proof of payment system.
average weekday subway ridership showed a year-over-year decline for the first time since the pandemic, albeit a nominal decline of 0.4%, with 3.89 million riders compared to 3.91 million in 2025
Customer Journey Time Performance was 58.7%, down 11.4 percentage points year-over-year and 13.4 percentage points month-over-month.

payload.raw_value_text: +50.4% increase in the Bus Collision Rate per Million Miles

evidence_refs.source_quote: Collisions increased by +42.5%, while mileage declined by -5.3%, resulting in a +50.4% increase in the Bus Collision Rate per Million Miles.
February 2026 ridership was also 2.3% above February 2025, consistent with the long-term upward trajectory in ridership
Customer Journey Time Performance (CJTP) was 83.0%
